tool_input.summary: Relevant Juno datasets in the PDS catalog for deriving electron/ion temperatures vs radial distance in Jupiter’s dawnside magnetodisk (use JADE moments/distributions for temperatures; SPICE for spacecraft radial distance and local time; MAG for magnetodisk/current-sheet context; JEDI for energetic ion/electron populations; Waves derived electron density for complementary plasma constraints).

candidates.title: JNO J JAD 5 MOMENTS V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Primary JADE (JAD) higher-level ‘moments’ product—this is the most direct PDS archive for electron/ion temperatures (and related moments) to bin against radial distance and select dawnside intervals.

candidates.title: JNO J SW JAD 3 CALIBRATED V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Calibrated JADE distributions useful if you need to recompute temperatures (e.g., separate proton vs heavy-ion populations via distribution fitting/assumptions) rather than relying only on provided moments.

candidates.title: JNO J SW JAD 5 CALIBRATED V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Another calibrated JADE archive level commonly used for science analysis and for deriving species-dependent temperatures from measured energy/angle distributions.

candidates.title: JNO J JED 3 CDR V1.0
candidates.reasoning: JEDI calibrated data product; supports analysis of hotter/energetic electron and ion populations that can be compared against JADE thermal temperatures and may provide heavy-ion information at higher energies.

candidates.title: JNO J 3 FGM CAL V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Juno MAG (FGM) calibrated magnetic field measurements; helpful to identify magnetodisk/current-sheet crossings and contextualize temperature changes with magnetic geometry on the dawn flank.

candidates.title: JUNO SPICE FILES
candidates.reasoning: NAIF SPICE kernels for Juno; needed to compute spacecraft position (radial distance in RJ), local time (dawnside selection), and coordinate transforms for mapping temperatures to magnetodisk geometry.

candidates.title: Juno Waves Electron Densities from Characteristic Plasma Wave Frequencies
candidates.reasoning: PDS4-derived electron density from Waves; complements JADE by providing an independent density constraint for dawnside magnetodisk intervals (useful for pressure/temperature consistency checks).

tool_calls.tool_output: Final result processed.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS catalog (atm, geo, img, naif, ppi, rms, sbn nodes, ~12k datasets).

Tools are provided via MCP. You MUST call at least one search tool before returning results.

Interpret the user's science question, infer the relevant mission(s) and instrument(s), then search using mission/instrument keywords — not raw science terms alone. Return both PDS4 and PDS3 versions when available. Only return candidates you've seen in tool results — never invent IDs. Stay under ~10 tool calls. If nothing matches, return an empty list.
How does temperature vary with radial distance for electrons, protons, and heavy ions in Jupiter's dawnside magnetodisk as observed by Juno?

parts.args: {"query": "Juno JEDI Jupiter magnetosphere ion electron temperature", "node": "ppi", "mission": "Juno", "instrument": "JEDI", "limit": 20, "fields": "summary"}
